title: May Jobs Report: 172K Payrolls — More Than Double Expectations; Rate Cut Hopes Fade
summary: 172K jobs added vs 80K expected. Unemployment 4.3% unchanged. Wage growth +0.3% MoM/+3.4% YoY. March+April revisions added +93K. Leisure/hospitality +70K (World Cup boost). Treasury yields jumped sharply higher. Rate cuts off near-term horizon per analysts. Q1 GDP 1.6% annualized; Atlanta Fed tracking ~3% Q2. Fed focused on persistent inflation, not labor weakness.

title: Fed Meeting Tracker: Next FOMC June 16-17; Polymarket 99% 'No Change'
summary: Current rate: 3.50-3.75%. Next FOMC June 16-17 (SEP included). March SEP: PCE 2.7% for 2026, GDP 2.4%, unemployment 4.4%. CPI jumped 2.4%→3.3% in March. Rate HIKE sentiment rising — 'for first time in years, prevailing sentiment is a rate hike much more likely than cut.' Polymarket: 99% no change at June meeting, 1% for 25bp cut. Powell: Iran war, tariffs pushing energy costs higher → feeds inflation.

title: SpaceX Targets Record $75B IPO at $135/Share — $1.75T Valuation, June 12 Debut
summary: Fixed price $135/share (no range). 555.6M shares, all primary. $75B raise — largest IPO ever (3x Alibaba). Ticker SPCX on Nasdaq, June 12. Musk retains 82% voting control. 2025 rev $18.67B, net loss $4.94B. Only Starlink profitable. P/S at 93.7x. 30% retail allocation. Roadshow started June 5. Morningstar fair value $780B — 48% below IPO price. xAI merger valued at $1.25T combined in Feb. Anthropic and OpenAI IPOs also expected.

title: Buy, Sell or Hold MSTR After Strategy's First Bitcoin Sale in Years?
summary: MSTR at $126, down 72% from July 2025 highs, -31% in last month. Q4 2025: $17.44B unrealized loss. Polymarket: 63% MSCI delisting probability by end 2026. Holding 843K BTC at $75,699 avg cost; current BTC ~$64K. Sale was tiny (0.0038% of holdings) but broke psychological contract. Verdict: HOLD. Next 1-2 weeks critical — BTC purchase announcement would restore confidence; another sale would signal structural shift.

title: Super Micro Closes $2B AI Infrastructure Deal with Gorilla Technology in India
summary: SMCI closed $2B AI infrastructure deal with Gorilla Technology (GRRR) for India. Covers 20,736 B300 cards, 5,120 B200 cards, networking equipment. SMCI up 34.5% YTD, ~20% last month, but -9.7% past week amid semis selloff. Helios AI factory platform launch. Analyst price targets $15-50.

title: IG Credit: AI Capex Boom Driving Record $2.25T Supply in 2026 — Spreads at 25-Year Tights
summary: IG spreads at levels last seen mid-1990s. AI capex driving unprecedented supply: $600B in 2026, $4T cumulative through 2030. Meta, Oracle, Google, Amazon raised >$100B combined in Q4 2025 alone. 2026 supply forecast $2.25T (+35% YoY). For first time since 2022, supply may outpace demand. Banks look compelling; technology warrants caution. Alpha from what you DON'T own. Selective, defensive positioning recommended.

title: Defense Stocks Hit Pause as Trump Pentagon Remakes Military Industrial Complex
summary: Defense stocks (RTX, LMT, NOC, GD) seeing mixed signals. Trump Pentagon remaking procurement priorities toward advanced warfare, missile defense, space-based systems. NATO allies increasing defense budgets. KeyBanc initiated coverage favoring 'new space' over legacy primes: RKLB, ASTS highlighted. Iran conflict should benefit defense but Trump's restructuring introduces uncertainty.
